Winner of the Warwick Award for Teaching Excellence (Social Sciences Faculty Award)
items.details: in recognition of an outstanding contribution to teaching and student support. The Social Sciences Faculty Award recognises the achievements of Warwick’s outstanding educators who have enabled excellent learning, creating the conditions within which all students are supported and empowered to succeed and thrive.

items.role: Fellowship of the Higher Education Academy
items.details: Awarded the Fellowship of the Higher Education Academy Academic during the 2023-2024 academic year upon completion of the Professional Pathway for Experienced Staff (APP EXP). This program, recognised by the Higher Education Academy, assists experienced staff in aligning with the UK’s Professional Standards Framework 2023 and in demonstrating their involvement in teaching and learning support in higher education.

items.role: Nominated, shortlisted as finalist, and awarded a commendation (3rd prize) in the Warwick Award for Teaching Excellence (WATE Social Sciences Faculty Award)
items.details: in recognition of outstanding contributions to teaching and the support of student learning.

items.role: Nominated and shortlisted as a finalist in the Student Teaching Award for Best Graduate Teaching Assistant
items.details: The award recognises teachers who have made an excellent contribution to the learning experience, inspired students to study beyond the classroom, and show clear passion and excitement for their subject matter.

items.role: Associate Fellowship
items.details: Certificate of Excellence in Teaching for passing the Developing as a Teacher in Higher Education course (DAT HE) during the 2018-2019 academic year: The DAT HE course has been developed in alignment with Descriptor I of the UK’s Professional Standards Framework for Teaching and Supporting Learning (Associate Fellowship).

Jury Member, Warwick Awards for Teaching Excellence
items.details: Evaluate university staff nominations for teaching awards and contribute to the recognition of excellence in teaching and student support.

items.role: Curriculum Review Lead
items.details: Led the review of undergraduate and postgraduate macroeconomics curricula. Conducted comprehensive curriculum mapping to identify gaps and overlaps across modules, and benchmarked programmes against competitor institutions to inform recommendations for curriculum development.

items.role: Panelist, Warwick Education Forum
items.details: Invited panelist at the Warwick Education Forum to contribute to discussions on teaching excellence and the future of education strategy at Warwick.

items.role: Coordinator, MSc Macro and Monetary Economics Dissertation Group
items.details: Support MSc students in developing dissertation topics, refining research questions, and identifying appropriate supervisors, while providing ongoing academic and pastoral guidance throughout the dissertation process.

items.role: GTA Assessor
items.details: Support the professional development of Graduate Teaching Assistants through teaching observations, structured feedback, and reflective discussions on pedagogical practice.

items.role: Coordinator, Teaching Focussed Staff Group
items.details: Support communication and engagement between TF colleagues and departmental leadership, organise meetings, coordinate evidence-based initiatives and feedback mechanisms, and contribute to the improvement of departmental culture and operations.

items.role: Exam Invigilation
items.details: Senior invigilator in main examination halls and invigilator for mitigating circumstances examinations.

items.role: Recruitment and Departmental Engagement Activities
items.details: Contribute to MSc and undergraduate recruitment and marketing activities through student feedback analysis, participation in Open Days, and attendance at graduation ceremonies.

items.role: Co-lead, Warwick Internship Scheme for Economists
items.details: Review and shortlist research internship proposals, support colleagues with recruitment processes, and participate in student interviews.

items.role: Student Support and Academic Service
items.details: Personal Tutor, providing academic guidance, mentoring, and pastoral support to approximately 27–30 students each year. Academic Referee, writing 25–45 comprehensive reference letters annually for current students and alumni.
